remeasurement.remeasurementRemindersItem2: Receive notifications when it's time to remeasure
remeasurement.remeasurementRemindersItem3: Never miss a scheduled monitoring session
remeasurement.identifyingTreesIntro: TreeMapper makes it easy to find trees that need remeasurement:
remeasurement.visualIndicatorsIntro1: All trees that are due for remeasurement will turn
remeasurement.visualIndicatorsIntro2: on the map. This visual indicator helps you quickly identify which trees need attention without having to check each one individually.
remeasurement.locationFeatureIntro2: to navigate to trees that require remeasurement. This is especially useful in large sites where trees may be spread across a wide area.
remeasurement.treesDueImageDesc: Map view showing trees highlighted in red that are due for remeasurement
remeasurement.consistentTimingDesc: Try to remeasure at the same time of year for comparable growth data
remeasurement.photoDocumentationDesc: Always include updated photos to visually track changes
remeasurement.noteConditionsDesc: Record any observations about tree health, pests, or environmental factors
remeasurement.regularScheduleDesc: Set up remeasurement reminders to maintain consistent monitoring

firstIntervention.title: Creating Your First Intervention
firstIntervention.description: Step-by-step guide to recording your first tree planting intervention in TreeMapper.
firstIntervention.intro2: in TreeMapper is any forestry action you document—such as planting trees, removing invasive species, or maintaining existing forests. This tutorial will walk you through creating your first tree planting intervention.
firstIntervention.beforeBeginItem1: Installed and set up the TreeMapper mobile app
firstIntervention.beforeBeginItem3: GPS signal (you should see your location on the map)
firstIntervention.beforeBeginItem4: Selected an active project (optional - only if you're signed in and want to upload to server)
firstIntervention.step1Intro1: Open the TreeMapper app and tap on the
firstIntervention.step1Intro2: tab at the bottom of the screen. You should see an interactive map centered on your current location.
firstIntervention.mapViewImageDesc: Screenshot of the TreeMapper map interface showing user location
firstIntervention.tip1Text: If you don't see your location, make sure GPS is enabled and you have location permissions granted. It may take a few seconds to acquire a GPS signal.
firstIntervention.step2Intro2: button at the bottom center of the screen to create a new intervention.
firstIntervention.addButtonImageDesc: Screenshot highlighting the + button to create new intervention
firstIntervention.step3Intro: Choose the type of intervention you want to create. TreeMapper supports multiple types:
firstIntervention.multiTreeDesc: For planting multiple trees in an area
firstIntervention.removalDesc: Removing invasive species or dead trees
firstIntervention.interventionTypesImageDesc: Screenshot showing intervention type selection screen
firstIntervention.step4Intro: After selecting the intervention type, mark where the intervention is taking place:
firstIntervention.pointLocationDesc: For a single tree, simply tap on the map where the tree is located. A marker will appear at that location.
firstIntervention.polygonAreaIntro: For planting multiple trees over an area:
firstIntervention.polygonStep1: Walk to the first corner of your planting area
firstIntervention.polygonStep2: Tap to place the first point
firstIntervention.polygonStep3: Walk to each corner, tapping to place additional points
firstIntervention.polygonStep4: Tap the first point again to close the polygon
firstIntervention.drawingPolygonImageDesc: Screenshot showing polygon drawing on map for multi-tree intervention
firstIntervention.proTip1Text: For accurate polygon mapping, walk slowly around the perimeter of your planting area. The app will track your GPS position automatically. You can edit the polygon later if needed.
firstIntervention.step5Intro: If your intervention type supports species selection (like Single Tree or Multi-Tree), specify which tree species you're planting:
firstIntervention.step5Item2: Search for the species by common or scientific name
firstIntervention.step5Item3: Select the species from the results
firstIntervention.step5Item4: Enter the number of trees being planted (for multi-tree interventions)
firstIntervention.speciesSelectionImageDesc: Screenshot of species search and selection interface
firstIntervention.note1Text1: If you can't find your species in the database, you can add a custom species or contact your project administrator. Learn more in the
firstIntervention.step6Note: Photo capture is only available for interventions that support tree planting (Single Tree or Multi-Tree) after you've selected the location.
firstIntervention.step6Intro: Photo documentation is crucial for monitoring progress over time. Take at least one photo of your intervention site:
firstIntervention.step6Item2: Take a photo of the planting area or tree
firstIntervention.step6Item4: Add more photos if desired (recommended: before, during, and after shots)
firstIntervention.photoCaptureImageDesc: Screenshot of camera interface for taking intervention photos
firstIntervention.step7Intro: For tree planting interventions, you can add measurements to track growth over time:
firstIntervention.treeHeightDesc: Height of the tree or sapling
firstIntervention.diameterDesc: Trunk diameter measurements (DBH - Diameter at Breast Height)
firstIntervention.plantingDateDesc: When the trees were planted (defaults to today)
firstIntervention.step8Intro: You can add custom tags to categorize and organize your interventions:
firstIntervention.tagsDesc: Custom labels for categorization (e.g., "monitoring plot", "restoration zone")
firstIntervention.interventionDetailsImageDesc: Screenshot of the intervention details form with tags and notes
firstIntervention.sampleTreesIntro2: interventions, TreeMapper supports the concept of
firstIntervention.sampleTreesIntro3: Sample trees are random tree data points within your planted location that you can register for long-term monitoring.
firstIntervention.exampleText: If you've planted 2000 trees in a multi-tree plantation with different species, you can register random sample trees (preferably of different species) from the location. Over time, you can monitor and remeasure these sample trees to assess the impact and growth of your restoration efforts.
firstIntervention.sampleTreesBenefit1: Representative monitoring without measuring every single tree
firstIntervention.sampleTreesBenefit2: Track growth and survival rates over time
firstIntervention.sampleTreesBenefit4: Scientific data collection for impact assessment
firstIntervention.sampleTreesHowTo: To add sample trees in a multi-tree intervention, select individual trees within your planted area and register them with specific measurements and photos. These sample trees will be marked for future monitoring visits.
firstIntervention.step9Intro: Once you've completed all the steps, your intervention is registered. You have two options:
firstIntervention.option1Desc: You can export your intervention data directly from the app without signing in. This allows you to share or backup your data locally.
firstIntervention.option2Title: Option 2: Save and Upload to Server
firstIntervention.option2Intro: If you're signed in, you can save the intervention and upload it to the server for:
firstIntervention.beforeSavingIntro: Before saving, review all the information you've entered:
firstIntervention.beforeSavingItem1: Check the location is correct on the map
firstIntervention.beforeSavingItem2: Verify species selection and tree count (if applicable)
firstIntervention.beforeSavingItem3: Ensure photos are clear and relevant (for tree planting interventions)
firstIntervention.reviewScreenImageDesc: Screenshot of intervention preview before registration
firstIntervention.offlineModeText1: TreeMapper works fully offline! You can create dozens of interventions in the field without internet. Your data is saved locally and can be exported or synced when you reconnect. Learn more about
firstIntervention.whatsNextIntro: Congratulations! You've created your first intervention. Here's what to explore next:
firstIntervention.whatsNextLink1Desc: to track tree growth over time
firstIntervention.whatsNextLink3: View your intervention on the web dashboard
firstIntervention.question1Title: Can I edit an intervention after creating it?
firstIntervention.question1Answer: Yes! Navigate to the Interventions tab, find your intervention, and tap to edit it. You can modify details, add more photos, or adjust the location.
firstIntervention.question2Title: What if I make a mistake?
firstIntervention.question2Answer: Don't worry—interventions can be edited or deleted. If you've already synced, contact your project administrator for help with deletions.
firstIntervention.question3Title: How do I know if my intervention synced?
firstIntervention.question3Answer: Check the Interventions tab. Synced interventions show a green checkmark, while unsynced ones show an orange cloud icon with "Pending Upload."

projectCreation.title: Creating a Project
projectCreation.description: Learn how to create a project in TreeMapper so all restoration activities are organized in one place.
projectCreation.intro2: is the core of restoration in TreeMapper. It's where all your restoration effort is organized—interventions, sites, monitoring plots, team members, and analytics.
projectCreation.intro3: You will be asked to create a project if you
projectCreation.intro4: in the mobile app or start using the web dashboard. This ensures your work is linked to the right project. You can create as many projects as you want.
projectCreation.createProjectImageDesc: Screenshot showing the create project flow
projectCreation.requiredDetailsIntro: When creating a project, you'll provide basic details such as:
projectCreation.name: Name
projectCreation.projectTypeDesc1: Select what type of project it is (choose
projectCreation.projectTypeDesc2: if it's only for personal use)
projectCreation.locationDesc: Optionally set the project location by selecting it on the map
projectCreation.projectDetailsImageDesc: Screenshot showing project name, type, and location selection
projectCreation.afterCreateIntro: Once saved, your project is created and you can start using other features like:
projectCreation.editingDesc: Project details can always be edited later from the
projectCreation.mobileVsWebIntro: Projects can be created from both the

siteCreation.title: Creating a Site
siteCreation.description: Learn how to create sites in TreeMapper to define where restoration activities take place.
siteCreation.intro: are the polygon areas where tree plantation (and other restoration activities) are going on. A single project can have as many sites as you want.
siteCreation.sitesOverviewImageDesc: Screenshot showing the sites list and create site button
siteCreation.creatingStep5: Define the site boundary (polygon) using one of the methods below
siteCreation.defineBoundariesIntro: You can define the site boundary in two ways:
siteCreation.drawPolygonTitle: 1) Draw a Polygon on the Map
siteCreation.drawPolygonDesc1: Draw the polygon over the map to mark the site boundaries. This is useful when you're in the field or when you already know the site area.
siteCreation.drawPolygonDesc2: Sites can also be created using the
siteCreation.drawPolygonDesc3: The advantage of creating a site with the app is that, if you don't have an accurate GeoJSON of the site, you can
siteCreation.traceWithGPS: trace the site using mobile GPS
siteCreation.drawPolygonDesc4: by moving across the border of the site and registering the boundary directly from the field.
siteCreation.drawPolygonImageDesc: Screenshot showing polygon drawing for site boundaries
siteCreation.uploadGeoJSONDesc: If you already have the site boundary in GIS format, you can upload a GeoJSON file for the site.
siteCreation.uploadGeoJSONImageDesc: Screenshot showing GeoJSON upload for site boundaries
siteCreation.accessManagementIntro: Sites also have access management. You can use this to give limited access to certain sites for specific team members.
siteCreation.accessManagementDesc: This is useful when a project includes multiple restoration areas and different teams should only work on specific sites.
siteCreation.nextLink1Desc: inside your site to start recording restoration work
siteCreation.nextLink2Desc: and assign access through roles and site permissions

monitoringPlots.title: Setting Up Monitoring Plots
monitoringPlots.description: Learn how to establish and manage monitoring plots for long-term forest research and tracking.
monitoringPlots.intro1: Monitoring Plots in TreeMapper are designed to help restoration teams systematically monitor a defined area over time. They enable users to assess how forests and landscapes change as a result of restoration activities and to compare areas with and without interventions.
monitoringPlots.intro2: Monitoring plots provide a structured way to track plant growth, survival, and environmental conditions, supporting long-term, evidence-based restoration monitoring.
monitoringPlots.whatIsDesc: A monitoring plot represents a specific, clearly defined area within a restoration site that is observed repeatedly over time. By collecting consistent data from the same area, users can understand trends in vegetation growth, species composition, and ecosystem recovery.
monitoringPlots.usefulForIntro: Monitoring plots are particularly useful for:
monitoringPlots.usefulForItem1: Measuring the effectiveness of restoration interventions
monitoringPlots.usefulForItem2: Comparing restored areas with untouched or naturally regenerating areas
monitoringPlots.creatingIntro: To create a monitoring plot, users complete a simple registration form. This form captures three key aspects:
monitoringPlots.plotTypeIntro: Users select whether the plot is an
monitoringPlots.interventionPlotsDesc: are areas where restoration activities (such as planting or assisted regeneration) have taken place.
monitoringPlots.controlPlotsDesc: represent areas without interventions and are used as reference points for comparison.
monitoringPlots.plotTypeImageDesc: Screenshot showing intervention vs control plot selection
monitoringPlots.plotDetailsDesc: Users define the basic characteristics of the plot, such as its name and dimensions. This ensures that the plot can be consistently revisited and monitored over time.
monitoringPlots.plotDetailsImageDesc: Screenshot showing plot name and dimensions input
monitoringPlots.locationDesc: The plot's geographic location is recorded so that it can be accurately mapped, revisited, and visualized within the platform.
monitoringPlots.locationImageDesc: Screenshot showing plot location on map
monitoringPlots.onceCompleted: Once these steps are completed, the monitoring plot is registered and ready for data collection.
monitoringPlots.addingPlantsTitle: Adding Plants to a Monitoring Plot
monitoringPlots.addingPlantsIntro: After a plot is created, users can add plant-level data within the plot area.
monitoringPlots.allowsPlotDesc: This allows the plot to capture both restoration efforts and natural regeneration in a single monitoring framework.
monitoringPlots.addingPlantsImageDesc: Screenshot showing plant data entry form
monitoringPlots.plantTimelineIntro: Plants within a monitoring plot can be observed repeatedly over time through a plant timeline.
monitoringPlots.asMonitoringItem1: Add new measurements as plants grow
monitoringPlots.asMonitoringItem2: Update existing measurements if corrections are needed
monitoringPlots.asMonitoringItem3: Mark plants as deceased when applicable
monitoringPlots.createsRecordDesc: This creates a clear historical record of each plant's development and survival, supporting long-term analysis of restoration outcomes.
monitoringPlots.plantTimelineImageDesc: Screenshot showing plant measurement history and timeline
monitoringPlots.plotObservationsIntro: Monitoring plots also include an observations section for recording broader environmental changes within the plot.
monitoringPlots.observationsRecordedDesc: Observations are recorded using a single, structured form and can be edited later if corrections are required. Additional observation types, such as bioacoustics, are planned for future updates.
monitoringPlots.mapViewIntro: Each monitoring plot includes a dedicated map view that shows:
monitoringPlots.mapViewItem2: The location of plants within the plot
monitoringPlots.mapViewDesc: From this view, users can navigate to individual plant details and adjust plot dimensions when necessary. This spatial overview helps ensure accuracy and clarity during field monitoring.
monitoringPlots.mapViewImageDesc: Screenshot showing plot boundary and plant locations on map
monitoringPlots.editingDesc: All monitoring plot data remains fully editable. Users can update plot details, plant records, observations, and locations to ensure data accuracy as monitoring progresses or new information becomes available.
monitoringPlots.plotGroupsIntro: Monitoring plots can be organized into groups to simplify analysis and management.
monitoringPlots.plotGroupsItem1: A group can contain multiple monitoring plots
monitoringPlots.plotGroupsItem2: Each monitoring plot can belong to only one group
monitoringPlots.groupsUsefulIntro: Groups are useful for organizing plots that share similar characteristics, objectives, or study designs, such as:
monitoringPlots.structureHelpsDesc: This structure helps users manage large monitoring efforts and analyze results across related plots.
monitoringPlots.whatsNextIntro: After setting up your monitoring plot, you can:
monitoringPlots.nextLink3: View plot data on the web dashboard

managingSpecies.title: Managing Species
managingSpecies.description: Learn how to manage tree species in TreeMapper on mobile and on the web dashboard.
managingSpecies.intro1: Species management is where you review, customize, and organize the species you use during intervention registration (for example during tree planting).
managingSpecies.intro2: TreeMapper downloads the species list during installation from the Plant-for-the-Planet database. It includes
managingSpecies.intro2Mid: covering the most planted species. Once downloaded, the species list remains available
managingSpecies.speciesListImageDesc: Screenshot showing the species list in the TreeMapper mobile app
managingSpecies.mobileAppDesc: Manage species directly on your device (works offline once downloaded)
managingSpecies.mobileAppTitle: Managing Species in the Mobile App (Offline-Friendly)
managingSpecies.mobileAppIntro: In the mobile app, species are available even without internet once they've been downloaded. You can customize species data locally to better match your field workflows.
managingSpecies.fromMobileIntro: From the mobile app, you can:
managingSpecies.fromMobileItem4End: so it's easy to find during planting registration
managingSpecies.editSpeciesImageDesc: Screenshot showing species details editing (local name, description, image, favorite)
managingSpecies.webDashboardTitle: Managing Species in the Web Dashboard
managingSpecies.webDashboardIntro1: You can also manage species from the web dashboard. This is especially useful when working with large lists, because the web dashboard provides
managingSpecies.webDashboardDesc: The same types of updates (like local naming, descriptions, images, and favorites) can be managed from the web dashboard as well.
managingSpecies.speciesNotFoundIntro: Plant-for-the-Planet maintains the core database of the most planted species. If you can't find a species you need, you can request it to be added.
managingSpecies.requestSpeciesDesc: To request a species, open the web dashboard and submit a request. The team will review it and add it to the database if appropriate.
managingSpecies.nextLink2Desc: to track planted and recruited plants over time

workingOffline.title: Working Offline
workingOffline.description: Learn how TreeMapper works completely offline, enabling data collection in remote locations without internet connectivity.
workingOffline.intro1: TreeMapper app works completely offline. This is to make sure that recording and other things work the same way in remote locations.
workingOffline.intro2: All core functionality is available without an internet connection, allowing you to collect data, take photos, and manage your restoration activities even in the most remote areas. The only time internet is required is when you want to upload data to the server for backup and use the web dashboard for better management and analysis.
workingOffline.offlineMapsIntro: User can download the maps for the area where they plan to do the restoration ahead of time. This will make sure map is available to use while recording trees at the time of restoration.
workingOffline.downloadMapsStep2: Enter the name of the location
workingOffline.downloadMapsDesc: TreeMapper supports many locations to be downloaded offline, so you can prepare multiple restoration sites before heading to the field.
workingOffline.offlineMapsDownloadImageDesc: Screenshot showing offline map download interface
workingOffline.tipText: Download maps while connected to Wi-Fi to avoid using mobile data. Map tiles can be several hundred megabytes depending on the area size.
workingOffline.offlineSpeciesIntro1: During the first time of app launch, TreeMapper would automatically download the species database which consists of 60K+ species. You can then manage it from the
workingOffline.refreshSpeciesDesc: You can always refetch/refresh the species by clicking on the refresh icon from the species database. This ensures you have the latest species information when you have internet connectivity.
workingOffline.speciesDatabaseImageDesc: Screenshot showing offline species database with 60K+ species
workingOffline.importantText: Once downloaded, the species database is always available offline. You can search, filter, and use all 60K+ species without any internet connection.
workingOffline.imageCollectionIntro: For the image capture of the trees, it's available offline and is stored locally. You can take photos of trees, interventions, and monitoring plots without any internet connection.
workingOffline.allPhotosItem3: Available for viewing and editing offline
workingOffline.allPhotosItem4: Automatically synced when you upload data to the server (if signed in)
workingOffline.offlinePhotoCaptureImageDesc: Screenshot showing camera interface for offline photo capture
workingOffline.exportDataIntro: After successful registration of trees, the data can be exported to GeoJSON format so it can be used in any ArcGIS supported device or other GIS software.
workingOffline.exportFunctionalityItem1: Export all your collected data without internet connection
workingOffline.exportFunctionalityItem2: Share data with other tools and platforms
workingOffline.exportFunctionalityItem4: Use data in external analysis tools
workingOffline.dataExportImageDesc: Screenshot showing data export options including GeoJSON format
workingOffline.monitoringPlotIntro: The component monitoring plot is offline based. Everything recorded is stored offline.
workingOffline.fullyUseIntro: You can fully use monitoring plots offline, including:
workingOffline.monitoringPlotDesc: All monitoring plot data is stored locally and will sync to the server when you upload your data (if signed in).
workingOffline.offlineMonitoringPlotImageDesc: Screenshot showing monitoring plot functionality available offline
workingOffline.whenInternetRequiredIntro: The only time TreeMapper requires internet is when you want to upload the data to the server to have a backup of it and use this data to better manage it using the web dashboard and also for analysis.
workingOffline.cloudBackupDesc: Upload your data to secure cloud storage for backup and access from multiple devices
workingOffline.webDashboardAccessDesc: View and analyze your data on the web dashboard with advanced analytics, reporting, and visualization tools
workingOffline.teamCollaborationDesc: Share data with your organization and work together on restoration projects
workingOffline.speciesDatabaseUpdatesDesc: Refresh the species database to get the latest additions
workingOffline.offlineMapDownloadsDesc: Download new map areas for future field work
workingOffline.noteText: You can work completely offline and export your data without ever signing in. Internet connectivity is only needed if you want to use cloud backup and web dashboard features.
workingOffline.whatsNextIntro: Now that you understand offline functionality, explore these guides:

teamCollaboration.title: Team Collaboration
teamCollaboration.description: Learn how to collaborate with your team in TreeMapper, manage roles, and invite team members.
teamCollaboration.intro1: TreeMapper supports team collaboration through role-based access control, allowing you to invite team members, assign appropriate permissions, and manage access to projects and sites.
teamCollaboration.roleManagementDesc: TreeMapper uses a role-based system to control what team members can do. Each role has specific permissions that determine access to features and data.
teamCollaboration.ownerDesc: Full control over the project including all administrative functions.
teamCollaboration.adminDesc: Project management capabilities with extensive permissions.
teamCollaboration.contributorDesc: role for field workers who collect data.
teamCollaboration.contributorItem2: Add and edit their own entries
teamCollaboration.observerDesc2: for viewing project data without making changes.
teamCollaboration.managingTeamDesc: Team management is done through the web dashboard, where you can invite members, assign roles, and manage access.
teamCollaboration.sendingInvitationsIntro: You can invite team members in two ways:
teamCollaboration.emailInvitationStep4: Select their role (Owner, Admin, Contributor, or Observer)
teamCollaboration.bulkInviteIntro: To invite multiple people at once:
teamCollaboration.bulkInviteStep3: Upload a CSV file with email addresses and roles
teamCollaboration.managingInvitationsDesc: You can manage pending invitations from the team management page.
teamCollaboration.viewPendingInvitationsDesc: See who has been invited and when
teamCollaboration.resendInvitationsDesc: Send reminder emails to pending members
teamCollaboration.updateRolesDesc: Change team member roles as needed
teamCollaboration.removeTeamMembersDesc: Remove members who are no longer part of the project
teamCollaboration.bestPractice1: Assign roles based on actual responsibilities
teamCollaboration.bestPractice2: Use Observer role for stakeholders who only need to view data
teamCollaboration.bestPractice3: Limit the number of Owners and Admins for security
teamCollaboration.bestPractice4: Regularly review and update team member roles
teamCollaboration.bestPractice5: Remove inactive members to keep the team list current
teamCollaboration.whatsNextIntro: Now that you understand team collaboration:
teamCollaboration.nextLink3Desc: to see team analytics and leaderboards

analyticsReporting.title: Analytics & Reporting
analyticsReporting.description: Learn how to use analytics and reporting features in TreeMapper to track progress and measure impact.
analyticsReporting.intro1: TreeMapper provides comprehensive analytics and reporting tools to help you track your restoration progress, measure impact, and share results with stakeholders.
analyticsReporting.overviewDashboardDesc: The overview dashboard provides a high-level view of your project's progress and key metrics.
analyticsReporting.speciesPlantedDesc: Number of unique species used in your restoration work
analyticsReporting.areaCoverageDesc: Total area covered by your interventions in hectares
analyticsReporting.numberOfContributorsDesc: Active team members contributing to the project
analyticsReporting.treesPlantedDesc: Total number of trees planted across all interventions
analyticsReporting.barGraphDesc: The bar graph visualizes tree planting activity over time:
analyticsReporting.barGraphItem4: Trend indicators showing growth or decline
analyticsReporting.treePlantationBarGraphImageDesc: Screenshot showing the tree planting bar graph
analyticsReporting.recentActivityDesc: The recent activity feed shows the latest updates in your project:
analyticsReporting.recentActivityDesc2: This keeps you informed about ongoing work without having to dig through data.
analyticsReporting.recentActivityFeedImageDesc: Screenshot showing the recent activity feed
analyticsReporting.targetTrackerIntro: The target tracker helps you monitor progress toward your planting goals:
analyticsReporting.targetTrackerItem1: Set planting targets for your project
analyticsReporting.settingTargetsDesc: You can set targets from the project settings. Targets help motivate your team and provide clear goals for restoration efforts.
analyticsReporting.downloadReportsIntro: You can download comprehensive reports from the dashboard:
analyticsReporting.reportUsesDesc: Reports can be used for stakeholder presentations, grant applications, compliance documentation, and external analysis in tools like Excel or GIS software.
analyticsReporting.upcomingFeaturesIntro: TreeMapper is continuously improving. Upcoming analytics features include:
analyticsReporting.aiIntegrationDesc: AI-powered insights and recommendations for restoration strategies
analyticsReporting.stayUpdatedDesc: Stay updated with the latest features by checking the release notes and documentation updates.
analyticsReporting.whatsNextIntro: Now that you understand analytics and reporting:

mapNavigation.title: Map Navigation
mapNavigation.description: Learn how to navigate and interact with maps in the TreeMapper mobile app.
mapNavigation.overviewDesc: The TreeMapper mobile app uses an interactive map as the primary interface for viewing and managing your restoration work. The map displays all your interventions, sites, and trees, allowing you to navigate to specific locations and record new data in the field.
mapNavigation.mapInterfaceImageDesc: Screenshot of the main map view showing interventions and sites
mapNavigation.mapViewsIntro: TreeMapper offers two map view options to suit different needs:
mapNavigation.standardMapViewDesc: The default map view shows roads, terrain, and landmarks. This view is useful for general navigation and understanding the landscape context of your restoration sites.
mapNavigation.satelliteViewIntro: Toggle to satellite view for aerial imagery of your sites. This is particularly helpful for:
mapNavigation.satelliteViewItem1: Identifying vegetation cover and land use patterns
mapNavigation.satelliteViewItem2: Verifying site boundaries against actual terrain
mapNavigation.satelliteViewItem3: Planning new planting areas based on existing vegetation
mapNavigation.satelliteViewDesc: Tap the satellite icon in the map controls to switch between standard and satellite views.
mapNavigation.interventionsDesc: Interventions appear on the map as markers (for point interventions) or shaded areas (for polygon interventions). The map uses clustering to group nearby interventions when zoomed out, making it easier to see the overall distribution of your work.
mapNavigation.clusteredMarkersDesc: When zoomed out, nearby interventions are grouped into clusters showing the count of interventions in that area
mapNavigation.individualMarkersDesc: Zoom in to see individual intervention markers
mapNavigation.polygonShapesDesc: Area-based interventions display as outlined polygons on the map
mapNavigation.sitesDesc: Project sites appear as polygon boundaries on the map, showing the defined areas where restoration activities take place. Sites help you understand the geographic scope of your project.
mapNavigation.sampleTreesDesc: When viewing a specific intervention, individual sample trees appear as markers within the intervention area. This allows you to see the distribution of planted trees and navigate to specific trees for remeasurement.
mapNavigation.sampleTreesImageDesc: Screenshot showing individual tree markers within an intervention
mapNavigation.selectingInterventionsDesc: Tap on any intervention marker or polygon to select it. When selected, the intervention details appear in a carousel at the bottom of the screen, showing:
mapNavigation.selectingInterventionsNote: Tap on the carousel card to open the full intervention details.
mapNavigation.userLocationDesc: Your current location is shown on the map with a location marker. The app also displays GPS accuracy information, helping you understand the precision of your location data. For best results when recording interventions, wait for the GPS accuracy indicator to show high accuracy (under 10 meters).
mapNavigation.pinchToZoomDesc: Use two fingers to zoom in and out
mapNavigation.dragToPanDesc: Slide one finger to move around the map
mapNavigation.doubleTapDesc: Quickly zoom in on a location
mapNavigation.zoomScaleDesc: A scale indicator shows the current zoom level for context
mapNavigation.filteringIntro: The map includes powerful filtering options to help you find specific interventions:
mapNavigation.filterByTypeDesc: Show only specific intervention types such as planting, maintenance, or monitoring interventions.
mapNavigation.filterByDateDesc: Set a date range to display interventions from a specific time period.
mapNavigation.filterByStatusDesc: Filter by completion status to find incomplete interventions that need attention.
mapNavigation.remeasurementFilterDesc: Show only trees that are due for remeasurement. Trees requiring remeasurement appear in red on the map, making them easy to identify and navigate to.
mapNavigation.mapFiltersImageDesc: Screenshot showing the filter options panel
mapNavigation.gpsAccuracyDesc: TreeMapper displays GPS accuracy information to help you collect precise location data. The accuracy indicator shows:
mapNavigation.highAccuracyDesc: Under 10 meters - ideal for recording interventions
mapNavigation.lowAccuracyDesc: Over 25 meters - consider waiting for better signal
mapNavigation.gpsAccuracyNote: For best results, ensure you have a clear view of the sky and wait for the accuracy to improve before marking locations.
mapNavigation.tip1Desc: Before going to areas with poor connectivity, download offline maps to ensure you can navigate even without internet
mapNavigation.tip2Desc: Switch to satellite view to better identify terrain features and existing vegetation
mapNavigation.tip3Desc: Always verify GPS accuracy before recording intervention locations
mapNavigation.tip4Desc: When working on specific tasks, use filters to reduce map clutter and focus on relevant interventions

creatingInterventions.title: Creating Interventions
creatingInterventions.description: Step-by-step guide to creating interventions in the TreeMapper mobile app.
creatingInterventions.overviewDesc: Creating an intervention in TreeMapper is a multi-step process that guides you through selecting the intervention type, marking the location, adding species information, and capturing photos and measurements. This comprehensive workflow ensures all necessary data is collected for each restoration activity.
creatingInterventions.interventionTypesIntro: TreeMapper supports over 13 intervention types to cover various restoration activities:
creatingInterventions.singleTreePlantingDesc: Plant one tree at a specific point location
creatingInterventions.multiTreePlantingDesc: Plant multiple trees within a defined polygon area
creatingInterventions.assistedRegenerationDesc: Document areas where natural regeneration is being supported
creatingInterventions.maintenanceDesc: General tree care like pruning, watering, or mulching
creatingInterventions.fireSuppressionDesc: Creating firebreaks or fire prevention measures
creatingInterventions.firePatrolDesc: Monitoring and patrolling for fire risks
creatingInterventions.step1Desc: From the main map screen, tap the add button to start creating a new intervention. This opens the intervention form where you'll enter the basic details.
creatingInterventions.newInterventionFormImageDesc: Screenshot of the initial intervention form
creatingInterventions.step2Desc: Choose the type of intervention you're performing from the list of available types. The selected type determines what additional information you'll need to provide.
creatingInterventions.step3Desc: Select which project this intervention belongs to. You can also optionally select a specific site within the project. For site-wide interventions, you can enable the "Entire Site" toggle to apply the intervention to the whole site.
creatingInterventions.step4Desc: The date picker allows you to set when the intervention was performed. By default, it's set to today's date, but you can adjust it if you're recording past activities.
creatingInterventions.step5Desc: You can provide a descriptive location name and any additional information about the intervention site. This helps identify the intervention later.
creatingInterventions.markingLocationDesc: After completing the initial form, you'll mark the intervention location on the map. The app supports two location types:
creatingInterventions.pointLocationIntro: Best for single trees or specific spot interventions:
creatingInterventions.pointLocationItem1: The map opens with your current GPS location
creatingInterventions.pointLocationItem2: Tap on the map to place the marker at the exact intervention location
creatingInterventions.pointLocationItem3: The GPS accuracy indicator helps ensure precise placement
creatingInterventions.pointLocationItem4: You can drag the marker to adjust the position
creatingInterventions.pointMarkerImageDesc: Screenshot showing point marker placement on the map
creatingInterventions.polygonLocationIntro: Best for area-based interventions like multi-tree planting:
creatingInterventions.polygonLocationItem1: Tap points on the map to define the boundary corners
creatingInterventions.polygonLocationItem2: Points are labeled alphabetically (A, B, C, etc.) for reference
creatingInterventions.polygonLocationItem3: Lines connect the points to show the polygon shape
creatingInterventions.polygonLocationItem4: Close the polygon by connecting back to the first point
creatingInterventions.gpsPolygonTrackingDesc: For accurate polygon mapping, use the GPS tracking feature to walk the perimeter of the area:
creatingInterventions.gpsPolygonTrackingItem1: Enable the polygon tracker to automatically record your path
creatingInterventions.gpsPolygonTrackingItem2: Walk slowly along the boundary of the intervention area
creatingInterventions.gpsPolygonTrackingItem3: The app records GPS points as you move
creatingInterventions.gpsPolygonTrackingItem4: Stop tracking when you return to the starting point
creatingInterventions.polygonTrackingImageDesc: Screenshot showing polygon boundary creation with GPS tracking
creatingInterventions.addingSpeciesDesc: For planting interventions, you'll be prompted to add species information. This can include:
creatingInterventions.speciesSelectionDesc: Choose from your project's species list or search the database
creatingInterventions.treeCountDesc: Number of trees planted for each species
creatingInterventions.sampleTreesDesc: Mark individual trees for detailed tracking
creatingInterventions.registeringSampleTreesIntro: Sample trees are individual trees you want to track over time. For each sample tree:
creatingInterventions.registeringSampleTreesItem1: Mark the exact location on the map
creatingInterventions.registeringSampleTreesItem3: Take a photo of the tree
creatingInterventions.registeringSampleTreesItem4: Optionally assign a tree tag ID
creatingInterventions.localDynamicFormsDesc: Depending on the intervention type, you may see additional form screens for specific data collection:
creatingInterventions.localFormsDesc: Project-specific fields configured by your organization
creatingInterventions.dynamicFormsDesc: Intervention-type-specific fields for detailed data capture
creatingInterventions.completingDesc: After filling in all required information, you'll see a preview screen showing all the collected data. Review the information and tap to complete the intervention. The intervention will be:
creatingInterventions.completingItem2: Synced to the server when you have internet connectivity
creatingInterventions.completingItem3: Visible on the map and in your intervention list
creatingInterventions.interventionStatusIntro: As you create an intervention, it progresses through these statuses:
creatingInterventions.tip1Desc: Wait for high accuracy before marking locations
creatingInterventions.tip2Desc: Capture the intervention site from multiple angles
creatingInterventions.tip3Desc: Walk slowly when using GPS tracking for accurate boundaries
creatingInterventions.tip4Desc: The app saves progress automatically, but complete interventions when possible
creatingInterventions.tip5Desc: You can create interventions without internet - they'll sync later

plotManagement.title: Plot Management
plotManagement.description: Learn how to create and manage monitoring plots in the TreeMapper mobile app.
plotManagement.overviewDesc: Monitoring plots are defined areas within your restoration sites used for systematic, long-term data collection. TreeMapper's mobile app provides comprehensive tools for creating plots, managing plot groups, and tracking individual plants within plots over time.
plotManagement.plotTypesIntro: TreeMapper supports two types of monitoring plots:
plotManagement.interventionPlotsDesc: Plots located within areas where restoration activities have been performed. Use these to monitor the success and growth of planted trees.
plotManagement.controlPlotsDesc: Plots in areas without intervention, used as a baseline for comparison. Control plots help you measure the impact of your restoration efforts by comparing treated and untreated areas.
plotManagement.plotShapesIntro: Plots can be defined in two shapes, each suited to different monitoring methodologies:
plotManagement.circularPlotsItem1: Defined by a center point and radius
plotManagement.circularPlotsItem3: Easy to establish in the field using a measuring tape from center
plotManagement.rectangularPlotsItem1: Defined by length and width dimensions
plotManagement.rectangularPlotsItem3: Easy to subdivide into smaller sampling units
plotManagement.rectangularPlotsItem4: Area calculated from length x width
plotManagement.plotShapesImageDesc: Diagram showing circular and rectangular plot configurations
plotManagement.plotComplexityIntro: When creating a plot, you can choose between two complexity levels:
plotManagement.standardPlotsDesc: Full-featured plots with comprehensive data collection options including nested subplots, detailed measurements, and environmental observations.
plotManagement.simplePlotsDesc: Streamlined plots for basic monitoring with fewer data fields. Ideal for quick assessments or when detailed monitoring isn't required.
plotManagement.creatingPlotIntro: Follow these steps to create a new monitoring plot:
plotManagement.step1Desc: Choose plot complexity (Standard/Simple), shape (Circular/Rectangular), and type (Intervention/Control)
plotManagement.step2Desc: Provide plot name, dimensions (radius or length/width), and any identification information
plotManagement.step3Desc: Use the map interface to draw or position your plot boundary
plotManagement.step4Desc: Take photos of the plot area for reference
plotManagement.step5Desc: Confirm and save the plot to begin data collection
plotManagement.plotCreationImageDesc: Screenshots showing the plot creation workflow
plotManagement.drawingBoundariesIntro: When marking the plot location on the map:
plotManagement.circularPlotsBoundaryDesc: Tap to set the center point, then adjust the radius
plotManagement.rectangularPlotsBoundaryDesc: Draw the corners or drag to position and resize
plotManagement.drawingBoundariesItem3: The app automatically calculates dimensions and area based on your drawing
plotManagement.drawingBoundariesItem4: You can manually adjust dimensions after drawing for precise measurements
plotManagement.managingPlantsIntro: Once a plot is created, you can track individual plants within it. Each plant record includes:
plotManagement.addingPlantsItem2: Tap to add a new plant
plotManagement.addingPlantsItem3: Select or search for the species
plotManagement.addingPlantsItem4: Mark the plant's location within the plot
plotManagement.plotGroupsDesc: Organize related plots into groups for easier management. Plot groups are useful when you have multiple plots that are part of the same monitoring design or sampling strategy.
plotManagement.creatingPlotGroupItem2: Create a new group with a descriptive name
plotManagement.creatingPlotGroupItem3: Add existing plots to the group
plotManagement.workflowDesc: Easily find and work with related plots
plotManagement.plantTimelineDesc: TreeMapper maintains a complete history of each plant in your plots. The plant timeline shows:
plotManagement.plantTimelineItem4: Status changes (if plant died or was damaged)
plotManagement.plotObservationsDesc: In addition to plant data, you can record environmental observations for each plot. These observations help document site conditions and factors that may affect plant growth.
plotManagement.editingPlotsDesc: You can update plot information including name, dimensions, and other details. Changes are tracked with timestamps for audit purposes.
plotManagement.deletingPlotsDesc: Plots can be deleted if no longer needed. Note that deleting a plot will also remove all associated plant records, so use this option carefully.
plotManagement.consistentNamingDesc: Use a systematic naming convention for plots (e.g., Site-Plot-Number)
plotManagement.accurateBoundariesDesc: Take time to precisely mark plot boundaries for consistent data collection
plotManagement.regularMonitoringDesc: Establish a schedule for revisiting plots to collect growth data
plotManagement.photoDocumentationDesc: Take overview photos of each plot during every visit
plotManagement.tagPlantsDesc: Use physical tags in the field to match digital records

measurements.title: Taking Measurements
measurements.description: Learn how to accurately measure trees using the TreeMapper mobile app.
measurements.overviewDesc: Accurate tree measurements are essential for tracking growth and assessing the success of your restoration efforts. TreeMapper provides tools for recording height, diameter, and other metrics with built-in validation to ensure data quality.
measurements.heightLengthDesc: Tree height is measured from the ground to the top of the tree. TreeMapper supports different units based on your location:
measurements.nonMetricCountriesDesc: Enter height in feet (automatically converted to meters for storage)
measurements.heightMeasurementImageDesc: Diagram showing how to measure tree height from ground to crown
measurements.diameterMeasurementsDesc: TreeMapper automatically determines which diameter measurement to use based on tree height:
measurements.dbhIntro: For trees taller than 1.3 meters (4.3 feet):
measurements.dbhItem1: Measured at 1.3 meters (breast height) above the ground
measurements.dbhItem2: Standard forestry measurement for established trees
measurements.basalDiameterItem1: Measured at the base of the tree, near ground level
measurements.basalDiameterItem2: Used for seedlings and young trees
measurements.appSwitchesDesc: The app automatically switches between "DBH" and "Basal Diameter" labels based on the height you enter, ensuring the correct measurement protocol is followed.
measurements.diameterMeasurementPointsImageDesc: Diagram showing DBH measurement at 1.3m vs basal diameter at ground level
measurements.takingMeasurementsIntro: When adding measurements to a tree, follow these steps:
measurements.step1Desc: Input the tree height in the appropriate unit for your region
measurements.step2Desc: Input DBH (for trees >1.3m) or basal diameter (for shorter trees)
measurements.step3Desc: Enable the tag switch and enter an alphanumeric tag ID if using physical tags
measurements.step4Desc: Capture a photo of the tree for visual documentation
measurements.step5Desc: Confirm and save the measurement record
measurements.measurementValidationDesc: TreeMapper includes built-in validation to help ensure your measurements are botanically reasonable:
measurements.heightToDiameterDesc: The app checks the ratio between height and diameter to identify potentially erroneous measurements. If the ratio falls outside expected ranges for typical trees, you'll see an alert.
measurements.validationFailsItem1: An alert appears explaining the potential issue
measurements.validationFailsItem2: You can review and correct your measurements
measurements.validationFailsItem3: You can choose to save anyway if the measurements are accurate for an unusual tree
measurements.inputValidationItem3: Empty values are not allowed for required fields
measurements.treeTagsDesc: Tree tags provide a physical-to-digital link for tracking individual trees in the field:
measurements.treeTagsItem1: Toggle the tag switch to enable tag input
measurements.treeTagsItem2: Enter an alphanumeric tag ID that matches the physical tag on the tree
measurements.treeTagsItem3: Tags must be unique within the intervention
measurements.treeTagsItem4: Use consistent tagging protocols across your project
measurements.treeTagInputImageDesc: Screenshot showing the tree tag input field
measurements.locationDataDesc: When you take measurements, TreeMapper automatically captures your current GPS location. This provides:
measurements.locationDataItem1: Geographic reference for the measured tree
measurements.unitConversionDesc: TreeMapper handles unit conversion automatically to ensure data consistency across all users:
measurements.useProperToolsDesc: A clinometer or hypsometer improves accuracy for tall trees
measurements.standAtKnownDistanceDesc: Position yourself at a consistent distance from the tree
measurements.identifyTheTopDesc: Clearly identify the highest point of the crown
measurements.accountForSlopeDesc: Measure from the uphill side on slopes
measurements.useDiameterTapeDesc: D-tapes give direct diameter readings from circumference
measurements.measurePerpendicularDesc: Hold the tape perpendicular to the tree trunk
measurements.avoidIrregularitiesDesc: If there's a bulge or damage at breast height, measure slightly above or below
measurements.recordMultipleStemsDesc: For multi-stemmed trees, measure each stem separately
measurements.beConsistentDesc: Use the same measurement technique across all trees
measurements.documentConditionsDesc: Note any factors affecting measurement accuracy
measurements.takePhotosDesc: Photos provide visual verification of measurements

photoDocumentation.title: Photo Documentation
photoDocumentation.description: Learn how to capture and manage photos in the TreeMapper mobile app.
photoDocumentation.overviewDesc: Photo documentation is a critical component of restoration monitoring. TreeMapper's integrated camera allows you to capture photos directly within the app, automatically linking them to interventions, trees, and plots for comprehensive visual records.
photoDocumentation.cameraFeaturesIntro: TreeMapper uses your device's native camera with optimized settings for field documentation:
photoDocumentation.realTimePreviewDesc: See exactly what you're capturing before taking the photo
photoDocumentation.autoFocusDesc: Automatic focus adjustment for clear images
photoDocumentation.metadataCaptureDesc: Automatic recording of image dimensions and properties
photoDocumentation.gpsTaggingDesc: Location coordinates captured with each photo
photoDocumentation.cameraInterfaceImageDesc: Screenshot of the TreeMapper camera interface
photoDocumentation.takingPhotosIntro: The photo capture workflow is straightforward:
photoDocumentation.step1Desc: The camera opens automatically when photos are needed during data entry
photoDocumentation.step2Desc: Position the camera to capture the tree, plot, or site clearly
photoDocumentation.step3Desc: Tap the capture button to take the photo
photoDocumentation.step4Desc: Review the captured image in the preview screen
photoDocumentation.step5Desc: Choose to continue with the photo or retake if needed
photoDocumentation.imagePreviewIntro: After capturing a photo, you'll see a preview screen with options:
photoDocumentation.continueDesc: Accept the photo and proceed with data entry
photoDocumentation.retakeDesc: Discard the photo and capture a new one
photoDocumentation.imagePreviewDesc: The preview includes loading states and error handling to ensure your photos are properly saved before continuing.
photoDocumentation.photoContextsIntro: TreeMapper captures photos in various contexts throughout the app:
photoDocumentation.speciesPhotosDesc: When adding species to an intervention, you can capture photos of the planted trees. These photos help document the species and condition at planting time.
photoDocumentation.sampleTreePhotosDesc: Each sample tree can have photos attached during initial registration and subsequent remeasurements. This creates a visual timeline of tree growth.
photoDocumentation.plotPhotosDesc: Monitoring plots can include overview photos showing the plot area, vegetation, and site conditions.
photoDocumentation.remeasurementPhotosDesc: When remeasuring trees, updated photos document current conditions and growth progress.
photoDocumentation.interventionPhotosDesc: General intervention documentation including before/after photos of the work performed.
photoDocumentation.photoTypesImageDesc: Examples of different photo types in TreeMapper
photoDocumentation.localStorageItem1: Photos are saved to your device's document directory
photoDocumentation.localStorageItem2: Organized by intervention ID or context
photoDocumentation.cloudStorageItem1: Photos sync to the server when internet is available
photoDocumentation.cloudStorageItem2: CDN URLs are generated for synced images
photoDocumentation.cloudStorageItem3: Original photos remain on device as backup
photoDocumentation.storageTrackingDesc: The app tracks image file sizes to help manage device storage and estimate upload bandwidth requirements.
photoDocumentation.photoDataIntro: Each photo in TreeMapper includes associated metadata:
photoDocumentation.latitudeLongitudeDesc: GPS coordinates where the photo was taken
photoDocumentation.fileSizeDesc: Size in bytes for storage management
photoDocumentation.uploadStatusDesc: Whether the photo has been synced to the server
photoDocumentation.cdnUrlDesc: Cloud storage URL after successful upload
photoDocumentation.fillTheFrameDesc: Get close enough to clearly show the subject
photoDocumentation.includeContextDesc: Show surrounding area for location reference
photoDocumentation.useReferenceObjectsDesc: Include a measuring stick or person for scale
photoDocumentation.holdSteadyDesc: Brace against something solid to avoid blur
photoDocumentation.cleanTheLensDesc: Wipe your camera lens before shooting
photoDocumentation.checkFocusDesc: Ensure the subject is sharp before capturing
photoDocumentation.reviewImmediatelyDesc: Check the preview and retake if needed
photoDocumentation.consistentStyleDesc: Use similar framing for comparison over time
photoDocumentation.beforeAndAfterDesc: Document conditions before and after interventions
photoDocumentation.captureIssuesDesc: Photograph any problems like disease or damage
photoDocumentation.overviewShotsDesc: Include wide-angle views of the entire site
photoDocumentation.photoBestPracticesImageDesc: Examples of good vs poor photo documentation
photoDocumentation.photoWontSaveItem1: Check that you have sufficient device storage
photoDocumentation.photoWontSaveItem2: Ensure the app has camera and storage permissions
photoDocumentation.photoWontSaveItem3: Try closing and reopening the camera
photoDocumentation.blurryImagesItem4: Wait for auto-focus to complete before capturing
photoDocumentation.photosNotSyncingItem2: Large photos may take longer to upload
photoDocumentation.photosNotSyncingItem3: Try syncing from the intervention list

offlineMaps.title: Offline Maps
offlineMaps.description: Learn how to download and use offline maps in the TreeMapper mobile app.
offlineMaps.overviewDesc: TreeMapper allows you to download map tiles for offline use, ensuring you can navigate and record data even in areas without internet connectivity. This is essential for fieldwork in remote restoration sites.
offlineMaps.howOfflineMapsWorkDesc: Offline maps use MapLibre's offline pack system to store map tiles directly on your device. When you download an area:
offlineMaps.howOfflineMapsWorkItem1: Map tiles for multiple zoom levels are saved to your device
offlineMaps.howOfflineMapsWorkItem2: The area name is determined automatically via reverse geocoding
offlineMaps.howOfflineMapsWorkItem3: Maps remain available until you delete them
offlineMaps.howOfflineMapsWorkItem4: You can have multiple offline maps for different regions
offlineMaps.step1Desc: Navigate to the Offline Maps section and tap to add a new offline map. The map view opens where you can pan and zoom to select the area you want to download.
offlineMaps.areaSelectionImageDesc: Screenshot showing map area selection for offline download
offlineMaps.step2Desc: Position the map view to show the area you need. The visible map bounds will determine what gets downloaded. Consider:
offlineMaps.step2Item1: Include all your restoration sites within the view
offlineMaps.step2Item2: Add some buffer around the edges for navigation
offlineMaps.step2Item3: Larger areas require more storage space
offlineMaps.step3Desc: Tap "Save Area" to begin the download. The app will show a progress indicator while downloading:
offlineMaps.downloadProgressImageDesc: Screenshot showing offline map download progress
offlineMaps.step4Desc: When the download completes, the offline map is saved with metadata including the area name and file size. You'll see a confirmation notification and the map will appear in your offline maps list.
offlineMaps.downloadSpecificationsIntro: TreeMapper downloads map tiles with the following specifications:
offlineMaps.zoomLevelsDesc: The zoom levels ensure you have detailed maps for precise work while keeping download sizes manageable.
offlineMaps.viewingDownloadedMapsIntro: Access your offline maps from the Offline Maps screen. Each map shows:
offlineMaps.areaNameDesc: Geographic location name from reverse geocoding
offlineMaps.sizeDesc: Storage space used by the map tiles
offlineMaps.offlineMapsListImageDesc: Screenshot showing the list of downloaded offline maps
offlineMaps.deletingIntro: To free up device storage, you can delete offline maps you no longer need:
offlineMaps.deletingItem2: Find the map you want to delete
offlineMaps.deletingDesc: Deleted maps are completely removed from your device. You can re-download the area later if needed.
offlineMaps.usingOfflineMapsDesc: Once downloaded, offline maps are automatically used when you're in the corresponding area without internet connectivity:
offlineMaps.mapNavigationDesc: Browse and navigate the map as usual
offlineMaps.creatingInterventionsDesc: Mark locations on the offline map
offlineMaps.plotManagementDesc: Create and view plots using offline tiles
offlineMaps.locationTrackingDesc: GPS continues to work independently of map tiles
offlineMaps.whenOnlineMapsDesc: When online maps aren't available and you don't have offline maps for an area, you'll see a basic map or no map tiles at all. GPS functionality remains available.
offlineMaps.mapDetailDesc: Areas with more features have larger tiles
offlineMaps.zoomLevelsDesc2: More zoom levels mean more tiles
offlineMaps.typicalSizeDesc: A typical restoration site area might require 50-200 MB of storage, while larger regions could require several hundred MB.
offlineMaps.managingDeviceStorageItem1: Download only the areas you need for upcoming fieldwork
offlineMaps.managingDeviceStorageItem2: Delete maps after completing work in an area
offlineMaps.managingDeviceStorageItem4: Keep some buffer storage for photos and intervention data
offlineMaps.downloadInAdvanceDesc: Download maps while you have good WiFi connection
offlineMaps.verifyCoverageDesc: Check that your downloaded area covers all planned work sites
offlineMaps.checkStorageDesc: Ensure you have enough space for maps and new data
offlineMaps.testBeforeGoingDesc: Verify offline maps work before losing connectivity
offlineMaps.useGpsWiselyDesc: GPS accuracy may vary; wait for good signal
offlineMaps.conserveBatteryDesc: Offline map use consumes less battery than streaming tiles
offlineMaps.syncDataDesc: Upload collected data when back online
offlineMaps.cleanUpDesc: Delete offline maps if no longer needed
offlineMaps.downloadUpdatesDesc: Re-download areas if map data has changed significantly
offlineMaps.downloadFailsItem3: Ensure you have sufficient device storage
offlineMaps.downloadFailsItem4: Restart the app and try again
offlineMaps.mapsNotDisplayingItem1: Verify the offline map covers your current location
offlineMaps.mapsNotDisplayingItem2: Check that the map wasn't accidentally deleted
offlineMaps.mapsNotDisplayingItem3: Zoom to a level within the downloaded range (14-20)
offlineMaps.slowDownloadItem1: Large areas take longer to download
offlineMaps.slowDownloadItem2: Use WiFi instead of cellular data for faster downloads
offlineMaps.slowDownloadItem3: Download during off-peak hours if possible

dashboardOverview.title: Dashboard Overview
dashboardOverview.description: Learn about the TreeMapper web dashboard and its key features for managing restoration projects.
dashboardOverview.overviewDesc: The TreeMapper web dashboard is your central hub for managing restoration projects, viewing analytics, and collaborating with your team. It provides comprehensive tools for data visualization, project management, and reporting.
dashboardOverview.dashboardHomeImageDesc: Screenshot of the main dashboard view
dashboardOverview.dashboardStatisticsDesc: The dashboard displays key performance indicators (KPIs) as cards at the top of the screen, giving you an instant overview of your project's progress:
dashboardOverview.treesPlantedDesc: Total number of trees planted across all interventions. Includes month-over-month comparison showing growth or decline trends.
dashboardOverview.speciesPlantedDesc: Count of unique tree species used in your restoration work. Tracks biodiversity in your planting efforts.
dashboardOverview.areaCoveredDesc: Total area of restoration interventions measured in hectares. Calculated from polygon interventions and site boundaries.
dashboardOverview.fieldDataCollectorsDesc: Number of team members actively contributing data to the project. Shows team engagement and capacity.
dashboardOverview.trendIndicatorsIntro: Each metric includes trend indicators showing:
dashboardOverview.directionDesc: Visual indicator showing increase or decrease
dashboardOverview.statisticsCardsImageDesc: Screenshot showing the KPI statistics cards
dashboardOverview.dateRangeFilteringDesc: Filter all dashboard data by date range using the advanced date picker. Available presets include:
dashboardOverview.dateRangeFilteringNote: The date filter applies to all dashboard components, allowing you to analyze data from specific time periods.
dashboardOverview.forestProgressDesc: The forest progress component visualizes your progress toward planting targets:
dashboardOverview.progressBarDesc: Visual representation of trees planted vs target
dashboardOverview.currentCountDesc: Number of trees planted so far
dashboardOverview.percentageCompleteDesc: How close you are to reaching your goal
dashboardOverview.forestProgressImageDesc: Screenshot showing the forest progress tracker
dashboardOverview.treePlantingChartDesc: The time-series chart visualizes tree planting trends over time:
dashboardOverview.historicalDataDesc: Compare current performance to past periods
dashboardOverview.recentAdditionsDesc: The recent additions component shows the latest activity in your project:
dashboardOverview.recentAdditionsNote: This keeps you informed about ongoing work without having to dig through data.
dashboardOverview.dashboardTabsIntro: The dashboard includes multiple views accessible via tabs:
dashboardOverview.overviewTabDesc: The main analytics view with statistics, charts, and progress tracking. This is the default view when you open the dashboard.
dashboardOverview.leaderboardTabDesc: Shows team member rankings based on contribution metrics. See who's planted the most trees and contributed the most interventions.
dashboardOverview.mapTabDesc: Geographic visualization of your project showing sites, interventions, and planted areas on an interactive map.
dashboardOverview.dashboardTabsImageDesc: Screenshot showing the dashboard tab navigation
dashboardOverview.roleBasedAccessDesc: Dashboard views vary based on your role in the project:
dashboardOverview.adminOwnerIntro: Full access to all dashboard features including:
dashboardOverview.dataExportDesc: From the dashboard, you can export your project data:
dashboardOverview.csvReportsDesc: Download intervention data in spreadsheet format
dashboardOverview.dateFilteringDesc: Export data from specific time periods
dashboardOverview.dataExportNoteEnd: for more details on export options.
dashboardOverview.navigationDesc: The dashboard sidebar provides quick access to all major sections:
dashboardOverview.gettingStartedIntro: When you first access the dashboard:
dashboardOverview.gettingStartedItem1: Select or create a project to work with
dashboardOverview.gettingStartedItem2: Review your dashboard statistics to understand current status
dashboardOverview.gettingStartedItem3: Set your planting target if not already configured
dashboardOverview.gettingStartedItem5: Begin creating sites and recording interventions

sitesManagement.title: Managing Sites
sitesManagement.description: Learn how to create and manage restoration sites in the TreeMapper web dashboard.
sitesManagement.overviewDesc: Sites are geographic areas within your project where restoration activities take place. The web dashboard provides comprehensive tools for creating, viewing, editing, and managing sites.
sitesManagement.viewingSitesDesc: The Sites page displays all sites within your current project. You can switch between different view modes:
sitesManagement.gridViewDesc: Visual card-based layout showing site previews with key information at a glance.
sitesManagement.listViewDesc: Tabular layout with sortable columns for detailed comparison and management.
sitesManagement.sitesListImageDesc: Screenshot showing the sites list view
sitesManagement.siteInformationIntro: Each site displays the following information:
sitesManagement.descriptionDesc: Details about the site and its purpose
sitesManagement.creator: Creator
sitesManagement.creatorDesc: Team member who created the site
sitesManagement.areaDesc: Size in hectares calculated from the GeoJSON boundary
sitesManagement.membersDesc: Team members assigned to the site
sitesManagement.searchingFilteringIntro: Find specific sites using the search and filter options:
sitesManagement.searchByNameDesc: Type to filter sites by name
sitesManagement.statusFilterDesc: Show all sites or filter by status
sitesManagement.sortableColumnsDesc: Click column headers to sort by name, creation date, etc.
sitesManagement.creatingNewSiteDesc: To create a new site, click the "Create Site" button to navigate to the site creation page.
sitesManagement.siteTypeDesc: Category of the site (Planting, Planted, Barren, Reforestation)
sitesManagement.definingBoundariesIntro: Site boundaries can be defined in two ways:
sitesManagement.drawOnMapDesc: Use the interactive map to draw a polygon boundary around your site. Click to add points and close the polygon to complete the boundary.
sitesManagement.uploadGeoJsonDesc: If you have existing GIS data, upload a GeoJSON file containing the site boundary polygon. The system will validate and import the geometry.
sitesManagement.siteCreationImageDesc: Screenshot showing the site creation interface with map drawing
sitesManagement.areaCalculationDesc: Once the boundary is defined, TreeMapper automatically calculates the site area in hectares based on the GeoJSON geometry.
sitesManagement.editingSitesDesc: You can edit existing sites to update their information:
sitesManagement.inPlaceEditingDesc: Click the edit button on a site to modify its details. Changes are saved when you confirm, or cancelled if you dismiss the edit mode.
sitesManagement.geometryUpdatesDesc: When you update the site boundary, the area is automatically recalculated to reflect the new geometry.
sitesManagement.siteAccessControlDesc: Manage who can access and work within each site. This is useful for large projects with multiple teams working in different areas.
sitesManagement.managingAccessItem2: Add or remove team members from the site
sitesManagement.managingAccessItem3: Save changes to update access permissions
sitesManagement.accessLevelsDesc: Site access works in conjunction with project-level roles. Team members with site access can work within that site according to their project role.
sitesManagement.siteAccessImageDesc: Screenshot showing the site access management modal
sitesManagement.deletingSitesDesc: Sites can be deleted if they're no longer needed:
sitesManagement.deletingSitesItem1: Click the delete option on the site
sitesManagement.deletingSitesItem2: Confirm deletion in the modal dialog
sitesManagement.deletingSitesItem3: The site and its associations are removed
sitesManagement.deletingSitesNote: Deleting a site does not delete interventions recorded within it, but those interventions will no longer be associated with a site.
sitesManagement.siteTypesIntro: TreeMapper supports different site types to categorize your restoration areas:
sitesManagement.plantingDesc: Areas actively designated for tree planting activities.
sitesManagement.plantedDesc: Areas where planting has been completed.
sitesManagement.barrenDesc: Cleared or empty areas awaiting restoration.
sitesManagement.reforestationDesc: Areas undergoing reforestation efforts, possibly including natural regeneration.
sitesManagement.namingConventionsItem1: Use descriptive, consistent names across sites
sitesManagement.namingConventionsItem3: Avoid special characters that may cause issues
sitesManagement.boundaryAccuracyItem1: Use GeoJSON files from surveyed data when available
sitesManagement.boundaryAccuracyItem2: Draw boundaries carefully, following actual site perimeters
sitesManagement.boundaryAccuracyItem3: Update boundaries if site areas change
sitesManagement.accessManagementItem1: Assign only necessary team members to each site
sitesManagement.accessManagementItem2: Review access periodically as team composition changes
sitesManagement.accessManagementItem3: Use site-level access for larger projects with distinct teams

bulkUpload.title: Bulk Data Upload
bulkUpload.description: Learn how to upload large amounts of intervention data using the TreeMapper web dashboard.
bulkUpload.overviewDesc: The bulk upload feature allows you to import large quantities of intervention data from spreadsheets or CSV files. This is useful when migrating data from other systems or recording historical planting data that wasn't captured in the mobile app.
bulkUpload.historicalDataDesc: Import past planting records not recorded in TreeMapper
bulkUpload.dataMigrationDesc: Move data from other forestry management systems
bulkUpload.largeDatasetsDesc: Upload hundreds or thousands of records at once
bulkUpload.speciesDataDesc: Bulk import species and tree information
bulkUpload.uploadWizardDesc: The bulk upload process uses a step-by-step wizard to guide you through the import:
bulkUpload.uploadWizardImageDesc: Screenshot showing the bulk upload wizard interface
bulkUpload.step1Desc: First, select the target project and optionally a specific site for the imported data:
bulkUpload.projectSelectionDesc: Choose which project will receive the uploaded data
bulkUpload.siteSelectionDesc: Optionally assign all uploaded interventions to a specific site
bulkUpload.step1Note: The system validates your selections before proceeding
bulkUpload.step2Desc: Upload your data file in CSV or spreadsheet format:
bulkUpload.fileParsingDesc: The system reads and parses your file structure
bulkUpload.columnMappingDesc: Match your file columns to TreeMapper fields
bulkUpload.previewDesc: See a sample of the data to verify it's correct
bulkUpload.fileUploadImageDesc: Screenshot showing the file upload interface
bulkUpload.step3Desc: The system validates your data before import:
bulkUpload.fieldValidationDesc: Check that required fields are present and properly formatted
bulkUpload.dataTypeVerificationDesc: Ensure dates, numbers, and coordinates are valid
bulkUpload.errorReportingDesc: Identify and display any issues with the data
bulkUpload.dataPreviewDesc: Review the validated data before confirming
bulkUpload.handlingErrorsItem1: The system displays which rows have issues
bulkUpload.handlingErrorsItem2: Error messages explain what needs to be corrected
bulkUpload.handlingErrorsItem3: You can go back to fix your file and re-upload
bulkUpload.handlingErrorsItem4: Some minor issues can be corrected in the preview
bulkUpload.step4Desc: After successful upload, you'll see a confirmation:
bulkUpload.nextStepsDesc: Option to start a new upload or return to the dashboard
bulkUpload.requiredFieldsIntro: Your upload file must include these minimum fields:
bulkUpload.optionalFieldsIntro: Enhance your data with additional fields:
bulkUpload.fileFormatGuidelinesItem2: Format dates consistently (ISO format recommended: YYYY-MM-DD)
bulkUpload.fileFormatGuidelinesItem4: Avoid special characters in text fields
bulkUpload.fileFormatGuidelinesItem5: Remove any summary rows or totals from the data
bulkUpload.bulkSpeciesUploadDesc: In addition to intervention data, you can bulk upload species information:
bulkUpload.progressTrackingDesc: The wizard shows your progress through the upload process:
bulkUpload.progressTrackingItem1: Visual step indicators show completed, current, and remaining steps
bulkUpload.progressTrackingItem2: You can navigate back to previous steps to make corrections
bulkUpload.progressTrackingItem3: Data persists across steps so you don't lose your work
bulkUpload.cleanYourDataDesc: Remove duplicates, fix formatting issues, and validate coordinates
bulkUpload.testWithSampleDesc: Try uploading a small subset first to verify the process
bulkUpload.backupOriginalDesc: Keep a copy of your original file before any modifications
bulkUpload.mapColumnsDesc: Understand how your columns map to TreeMapper fields
bulkUpload.reviewCarefullyDesc: Check the data preview at each step
bulkUpload.addressErrorsDesc: Don't ignore validation warnings - fix issues in your source file
bulkUpload.dontRefreshDesc: Avoid refreshing the page during upload

planetPlatformIntroduction.title: Planet Platform - Introduction
planetPlatformIntroduction.description: Learn about the Planet Platform and how organizations can receive donations for their restoration projects.
planetPlatformIntroduction.content: The Restoration Platform, the core of Forest Cloud, transforms global restoration by simplifying fundraising and ensuring transparency for forest conservation. Since 2018, it has evolved to bring back a trillion trees and conserve 3 trillion existing trees. The open-source, open access, digital solution currently supports scientific restoration initiatives as well as managing and coordinating conservation and restoration efforts globally. It benefits from robust Restoration Standards, proprietary restoration monitoring applications, a robust peer-review system and an ecosystem of ancillary digital solutions - the 'Forest Cloud'. Proven across 300 restoration initiatives and growing, the Platform unifies restoration organizations (ROs), donors, and scientists. Having demonstrated its success by restoring over 100M trees in 6 years, we now seek to improve its scale and geographic reach and solve the logistical challenges to contribute to a sustainable, thriving future.
planetPlatformIntroduction.platformScreenshotDesc: Screenshot of the Planet Platform showing the project listing and donation interface
planetPlatformIntroduction.whatIsContent: The Planet Platform connects restoration organizations with donors worldwide, creating opportunities for funding forest restoration and tree planting projects. We provide comprehensive support including advisory services, project management tools, and access to a global network of supporters committed to environmental restoration.
planetPlatformIntroduction.statsContent: We have projects from 70+ countries and more than 200+ projects listed on the platform, representing a diverse global network of restoration initiatives working towards a common goal of reforestation and ecosystem restoration.
planetPlatformIntroduction.feature1: Access to global donor network for project funding
planetPlatformIntroduction.feature2: Advisory services and guidance for restoration projects
planetPlatformIntroduction.feature4: Platform visibility to showcase your restoration work
planetPlatformIntroduction.integrationContent: If you're an organization working on restoration projects and looking for funding opportunities, the Planet Platform can help connect you with donors and provide the tools you need to manage and showcase your work effectively.
planetPlatformIntroduction.nextStep2: Review required documents and contact information

planetPlatformRegistration.title: Planet Platform - Registration Process
planetPlatformRegistration.description: Step-by-step guide to registering your organization on the Planet Platform.
planetPlatformRegistration.intro: To get started with the Planet Platform, you'll need to complete the registration process. This ensures that your organization meets the requirements and can access all available resources and tools.
planetPlatformRegistration.registrationLinkText: The registration for the platform can be accessed via
