planetPlatformRegistration.step1Description: Fill out your organization's information including name, location, contact details, and project description. Provide accurate and complete information to ensure a smooth registration process.
planetPlatformRegistration.step2Description: Upload all required documents as specified in the registration form. This may include organization certificates, project proposals, and other supporting materials. Ensure all documents are clear and up-to-date.
planetPlatformRegistration.step3Description: The Planet Platform team will review your application and verify all submitted information and documents. This process ensures that all organizations on the platform meet quality standards and requirements.
planetPlatformRegistration.step4Description: Once your registration is approved and verified, your organization and projects will be listed on the Planet Platform, making them visible to potential donors and supporters worldwide.
planetPlatformRegistration.approvalContent: Once you've submitted your registration form and all required documents, the Planet Platform team will review your application. The approval process typically takes 3-5 business days. You will receive an email notification once your registration has been approved, along with instructions on how to access the platform.
planetPlatformRegistration.noteContent: Make sure all information provided is accurate and all required documents are included. Incomplete applications may delay the approval process. Selecting the correct organization type (RO) during registration is essential.

planetPlatformDocuments.title: Planet Platform - Documents Required & Contact
planetPlatformDocuments.description: Learn about the documents required for registration and how to contact the Planet Platform team.
planetPlatformDocuments.verificationIntro: Restoring forests is a complex and long-term endeavor that requires a holistic approach. To address this, Plant-for-the-Planet has developed its own minimum and top-tier 'Standards' for various restoration and conservation methods (e.g. reforestation, agroforestry, and assisted natural regeneration) that consider biological, social, and economic factors. Organizations and their projects undergo a rigorous verification process during onboarding and are evaluated against these 'Standards'. Only those meeting the minimum 'Standards' are featured on the platform and allowed to actively collect donations.
planetPlatformDocuments.verificationFirstStage: At first stage, the proposals are reviewed by in-house expertise.
planetPlatformDocuments.verificationSecondStage: In the second phase of due diligence, external experts visit the projects for on-site evaluations based on the established standards. These evaluation results are then reviewed, discussed, and ultimately assessed by an independent, voluntary panel of experts - the so-called Review Board.
planetPlatformDocuments.verificationConclusion: This thorough process ensures that only high-quality, impactful projects are showcased and supported through our platform, fostering trust and delivering meaningful results. At the same time, Plant-for-the-Planet remains committed to continuously questioning, reviewing, and refining these standards, integrating the latest scientific research findings to ensure their effectiveness and relevance.
planetPlatformDocuments.documentsIntro: To complete your registration on the Planet Platform, you will need to provide the following documents:
planetPlatformDocuments.document1: Organization registration certificate or legal documentation proving your organization's legal status
planetPlatformDocuments.document2: Proof of project location and land ownership or usage rights (e.g., land title, lease agreement, or usage permit)
planetPlatformDocuments.document3: Project proposal or detailed description of your restoration activities, including goals, timeline, and expected outcomes
planetPlatformDocuments.document4: Contact information for your organization's primary representative, including name, title, email, and phone number
planetPlatformDocuments.documentsNote: All documents should be submitted in PDF format. If documents are not in English, please provide certified translations. Ensure all documents are clear, legible, and up-to-date.
planetPlatformDocuments.contactIntro: For questions about the Planet Platform, assistance with registration, or general inquiries, please contact us using the following information:
planetPlatformDocuments.contactNote: Our support team is available Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM CET. For urgent matters, please include 'URGENT' in your email subject line. We aim to respond to all inquiries within 2-3 business days.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Find answers to common questions about TreeMapper.
faq.intro: Browse our most frequently asked questions below. Can't find what you're looking for?
faq.supportTeamIntro: Our support team is here to help. Reach out directly for assistance.

whatIsTreeMapper.question: What is TreeMapper?
whatIsTreeMapper.answer: TreeMapper is a comprehensive tree planting and forest restoration tracking platform. It consists of a mobile app for field data collection and a web dashboard for project management, analytics, and reporting. TreeMapper helps organizations document planting activities, monitor tree growth, and report on restoration progress.

isFree.question: Is TreeMapper free to use?
isFree.answer: Yes, TreeMapper is free for restoration projects. The mobile app can be downloaded from the App Store (iOS) or Google Play Store (Android), and the web dashboard is accessible at no cost. TreeMapper is developed by Plant-for-the-Planet to support global reforestation efforts.

devicesSupported.question: What devices are supported?
devicesSupported.answer: The TreeMapper mobile app works on iOS devices (iPhone and iPad) and Android devices. The web dashboard works on any modern web browser including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge on desktop and laptop computers.

offlineUse.question: Can I use TreeMapper offline?
offlineUse.answer: Yes! TreeMapper is designed for field use where internet connectivity may be limited. You can create interventions, take measurements, and capture photos completely offline. All data is stored locally on your device and automatically syncs to the server when you regain internet connectivity. You can also download offline maps for navigation in remote areas.

gpsAccuracy.question: How accurate is the GPS in TreeMapper?
gpsAccuracy.answer: GPS accuracy depends on your device and environmental conditions. TreeMapper displays a GPS accuracy indicator so you know the precision of your location data. For best results, wait for the accuracy to show under 10 meters before marking locations. Standing in open areas with clear sky visibility improves accuracy.

deleteApp.question: What happens if I delete the app?
deleteApp.answer: Synced interventions are safely stored on the server and won't be lost. However, any unsynced interventions stored only on your device will be permanently deleted. Always sync your data before uninstalling the app or switching devices to ensure nothing is lost.

offlineLimit.question: How many interventions can I create offline?
offlineLimit.answer: There's no practical limit to offline interventions. You can create hundreds of interventions offline, limited only by your device storage (primarily due to photos). Each intervention without photos uses only about 1KB of data. Photos are the main storage consideration.

syncData.question: How do I sync my data?
syncData.answer: Data syncs automatically when your device has an internet connection. You can also manually trigger a sync from the interventions list. The app shows sync status for each intervention so you know which ones have been uploaded and which are pending.

editAfterSync.question: Can I edit an intervention after syncing?
editAfterSync.answer: Yes, you can edit most fields like notes, species, and measurements after syncing. However, changing the location or intervention type may require administrator approval depending on your project settings. Edits are tracked for audit purposes.

exportData.question: How do I export my data?
exportData.answer: From the mobile app, you can export individual interventions as GeoJSON files for use in GIS software like ArcGIS. From the web dashboard, you can export all your data in Excel format, with options to filter by date range. Species data and team data can also be exported from their respective sections.

projectVsSite.question: What is the difference between a project and a site?
projectVsSite.answer: A project is the overall restoration initiative that contains all related activities, team members, and analytics. A site is a specific geographic area (polygon) within a project where planting and other activities take place. One project can have many sites.

createSiteBoundary.question: How do I create a site boundary?
createSiteBoundary.answer: You can create site boundaries in three ways: (1) Draw a polygon directly on the map in the web dashboard, (2) Upload a GeoJSON file if you have existing GIS data, or (3) Use the mobile app's GPS tracking to walk the perimeter and trace the boundary. The area is calculated automatically from the boundary.

userRoles.question: What are the different user roles?
userRoles.answer: TreeMapper has four roles: Owner (full control including deletion), Admin (manage team, sites, and settings), Contributor (create and edit interventions), and Observer (read-only access). Each role has specific permissions suited to different team member responsibilities.

inviteTeam.question: How do I invite team members?
inviteTeam.answer: From the web dashboard, go to Team Management and click Invite. Enter the person's email address, select their role, and send the invitation. They'll receive an email with a link to join your project. You can also bulk invite multiple people using a CSV file.

speciesCount.question: How many species are available?
speciesCount.answer: TreeMapper includes over 60,000 species from the Plant-for-the-Planet database, covering the most commonly planted species worldwide. The species list is downloaded during app installation and available offline. If you need a species that's not in the database, you can request it to be added.

unknownSpecies.question: What if I cannot identify a species in the field?
unknownSpecies.answer: You can record an "unknown species" during field work. Later, from the web dashboard, you can review unknown species and assign them to the correct scientific species once identified. This maintains data integrity while allowing field work to continue without delays.

trackGrowth.question: How do I track tree growth over time?
trackGrowth.answer: Use the remeasurement feature to record updated measurements for trees. Select the tree, choose "Measure," and enter new height and diameter values with a photo. All measurements are stored as a timeline history. You can also set up remeasurement reminders and see trees due for remeasurement highlighted in red on the map.

monitoringPlots.question: What are monitoring plots?
monitoringPlots.answer: Monitoring plots are defined areas (circular or rectangular) used for systematic, long-term data collection. They can be intervention plots (in restored areas) or control plots (for comparison). Plots help you track individual plants, measure growth, and assess restoration success over time.
